Dodge Scoot.description: This is an electric-powered scooter with a top speed of don't make me laugh. It's compact, fuel-efficient, and super-affordable, but no self-respecting shadowrunners would be caught dead on one.

Harley-Davidson Scorpion.description: This bike is a classic, heavy-bodied, heavily chromed road hog, armored and styled for intimidation. Very popular with a certain type of go-ganger, and with big, tough orks and trolls who don't worry about little things like embodying a cliché. This bike is even tough enough to be used in the combat biking circuit.

Yamaha Growler.description: This is a durable off-road bike used in motocross and other extreme sports events, usually seen in photos and footage with both the bike and its rider covered in dust. It's popular with thrill seekers, wilderness buffs, and light-freight drug smugglers.

Suzuki Mirage.description: This Japanese racing bike is a fast, sleek speed machine, popular with the other type of go-ganger. Both the Scorpion and the Mirage are popular with the Cutters, while the Ancients show a notable preference for the Mirage and its cousins and competitors. Like the Scorpion, the Mirage also sees some use in the combat bike circuit.

Chrysler-Nissan Jackrabbit.description: This cheap, ubiquitous electric subcompact was made in North America for decades. Even after North American production ceased, dozens of generic rip-offs were contract produced throughout the Third World. The sheer number of Chrysler-Nissan Jackrabbits on the road means the older model must continue to be supported.

Honda Spirit.description: This sleek three-wheeled two-seater features an electric engine and is popular with commuting sprawl dwellers. The Spirit has a bubble hood for bad weather, with a chemical coating guaranteed to neutralize 89 percent of acid rain.

Hyundai Shin-Hyung.description: This cheap four-door sports sedan is a fast, powerful car for corp youth to show off their moxie to friends in a socially acceptable and constructive way. Available for low prices out of the box, the Shin-Hyung is often heavily customized by tuners and street-racing enthusiasts until it is far more functional and barely recognizable. It is popular with Triad and Yakuza elements, more affluent street gangs, and slumming rich kids.

Eurocar Westwind 3000.description: This sleek, classic, low-slung speed machine mixes business and pleasure into perfection. Situated at the intersection of luxury and performance, this turbo-charged sports car is only a pipe dream for most slags working their way up through the shadows. Its sexy lines and powerful engine make it consistently popular among muscle-car enthusiasts.

Ford Americar.description: This four-door sedan has an affordable price point, capacious luggage storage, and safety features that made it the bestselling family car in North America for decades before it lost out to the Mercury Comet. The Americar is reliable and economical, though neither fast nor sexy. It is a straightforward and anonymous way to move four metahumans from point A to point B.

Saeder-Krupp-Bentley Concordat.description: This luxury sedan is stylish, stately, and impressive. It is fast and powerful, comfortable and safe, opulently appointed, and most importantly a status symbol. The Concordat is the kind of car that simstars and orxploitation rappers drive; a runner behind the wheel of one has made the big time indeed. Either that, or they stole it.

Mitsubishi Nightsky.description: Decadent, secure, and high-tech, this armored limousine is intended for corp executives, media stars, and Mr. Johnson. Only in their company are most runners likely to enjoy its spacious interior and luxurious amenities.

Toyota Gopher.description: This is a classic pickup with great off-road suspension, superior handling, and a loyal customer base. The low-maintenance design is a bit dated but sees widespread use in Third World nations and rural areas, especially the NAN. Its powerful engine and storage space are useful for heavy-duty hauling.

GMC Bulldog Step-Van.description: This long-running top seller from GM is a capacious armored delivery van favored by everyone from legitimate shipping and logistics companies to security agencies, heavy mob crews, and shadowrunners. It has plenty of room for people, gear, or whatever needs moving, plus an engine and chassis that can take substantial punishment and keep running.

Rover Model 2072.description: Bigger and more powerful than it needs to be, this luxury van is the top of the line in its class and as much a status symbol as the Bentley. It packs intimidating size and all the accessories for cruising through the wilderness, though it is more commonly seen in elite urban neighborhoods. Its combination of status and security has made it increasingly popular with discreet security details.

Ares Roadmaster.description: This is an armored car, legitimately used to move goods over land that need secure transportation. The Roadmaster has the speed and handling of the tank that it resembles and is anything but subtle, but it can reliably shrug off small-arms fire and even grenades. For many teams, that is a worthwhile tradeoff.

Samuvani Criscraft Otter.description: This midsize craft is mainly used for pleasure boating, fishing, light hauling, and utility work. A five-meter-long vessel with a fiberglass open hull, the Otter is a well-rounded, general-purpose boat that does not stand out in any one area.

Yongkang Gala Trinity.description: This small, fast-moving speedboat is known as a smuggler's craft. It is generally built on the spot from prepared components and a hull kit, with its payload built directly into the hull. On arrival, the Trinity is taken apart around its cargo, with the profit from the haul compensating for the fact that the boat was used only once.

Morgan Cutlass.description: This commonly seen patrol boat comes equipped with a pair of heavy, manually operated weapon mounts, usually outfitted with light machine guns, and a high-quality sensor suite.
features: Includes two heavy weapon mounts with manual operation.

Proteus Lamprey.description: This underwater sea-sled is used for underwater inspection and transportation, with riding positions for up to four scuba-equipped passengers. Although closest to a submarine, the Lamprey does not protect passengers from the rigors of the deep. Military models are used for aquatic insertions by specially equipped naval special-operations forces. The Lamprey also includes a drone rack for one seaworthy drone of medium size or smaller, allowing exploration at depths too dangerous for its passengers.
features: Includes a drone rack for one seaworthy drone of medium size or smaller.
Does not protect passengers from deep-water pressure or other environmental hazards.

Vulkan Electronaut.description: This two-person mini-sub is the cheapest available in its class and comes in a wide selection of utility models. It is often used for patrols, short-range reconnaissance, and resupply runs, but can also be leveraged by shadowrunner teams looking for unexpected ways to approach nautical targets.

Artemis Industries Nightwing.description: The Nightwing is an older-model ultralight closely resembling designs from the late twentieth century. It has a shrouded electric motor to minimize aircraft sound and reduce heat emissions. Although intended to prevent noise pollution and environmental impact, this also makes the small glider-like craft quite stealthy. It is popular with rural aviation enthusiasts, smugglers, and anyone who favors a classic stealth insertion.

Cessna C750.description: This dual-prop civilian plane can carry passengers, haul light cargo, or serve as a surveillance plane. Its light frame, economy, affordable price point, and commonality have ensured that some see use in smuggling operations.

Renault-Fiat Fokker Tundra-9.description: This amphibious jet is suited to heavy cargo and personnel hauling and does not need an airfield to land. It is used by corporate logistics teams to resupply out-of-the-way facilities and by Awakened game hunters and poachers to reach dangerous game. It comes standard with buoyancy and flotation upgrades, allowing it to take off from land and water.
features: Amphibious; can take off from and land on both land and water.

Ares Dragon.description: This versatile, solidly built, double-prop cargo helicopter is used in a wide variety of roles in urban and remote areas across the Sixth World. It often performs heavy cargo-hauling operations and occasionally serves in a military capacity as a hybrid gunship and troop transport for Knight Errant and their elite Firewatch special forces.

Nissan Hound.description: This bleeding-edge transport helicopter is used by the Imperial Japanese Marines and special-forces units around the globe. It is also popular with corporate-security rapid-response forces, especially those belonging to Japanacorps such as the Renraku Red Samurai. It comes armed with two weapon mounts to ensure that its precious cargo survives hot dustoffs and difficult landings.

Northrup Wasp.description: This one-man rotorcraft is designed for police and security service. A Wasp, lightly armored and equipped with a heavy weapon mount, is the standard corporate-security response to ever-mounting shadowrunner problems.

Ares Venture.description: This small, cheap LAV is manufactured from off-the-shelf parts at environmentally friendly plants and is used by smugglers worldwide. It also sees a wide range of legitimate, security, and military use. The Venture is often used for cargo or passenger transport, and heavily armed and armored variants exist for patrol duty. It sells well among Third World customers.

GMC Banshee.description: This lightning-fast, supersonic aircraft is the classic iconic t-bird, with the armor, armaments, and sensor suites to prove it. The Banshee sees military use in reconnaissance and courier duty and is a favorite of elite smugglers for white-knuckle illegal border crossings, using its ability to fly under radar through canyons at high speed.

Federated Boeing Commuter.description: This tilt-wing passenger aircraft sees heavy use as an inter-city shuttle, hopping between corporate-controlled aerospaceports and ferrying sararimen around the world. Shadowrunners are unlikely to use one as personal equipment, but may encounter it during extraction or executive-protection work.

category.Groundcraft:
Vehicle Tests
Groundcraft use Pilot Ground Craft + Reaction [Handling] when manoeuvrability is the main concern. When raw speed is the focus, use Speed as the test limit instead. Routine driving normally requires no test; hazardous manoeuvres use the Vehicle Test thresholds and terrain modifiers.

Road and Off-Road Ratings
Where a groundcraft lists Handling or Speed as two values separated by a slash, the first value is used On Road and the second Off Road.

Travel
With GridGuide, average travel is about 80 km/h in urban areas and 120 km/h on rural or cross-country routes, subject to roads, traffic, terrain, and local conditions.
category.Watercraft:
Vehicle Tests
Watercraft use Pilot Watercraft + Reaction [Handling] when manoeuvrability is the main concern. When raw speed is the focus, use Speed as the test limit instead. Routine travel normally requires no test; dangerous manoeuvres, rough water, poor visibility, and similar hazards can require Vehicle Tests.

Travel
Currents, the shape of the body of water, and the need to follow navigable routes can change effective travel time. As broad Core Rulebook averages, small powerboats cruise at about 25 km/h, larger powerboats at about 65 km/h, and speedboats at about 130 km/h.
category.Aircraft:
Vehicle Tests
Aircraft use Pilot Aircraft + Reaction [Handling] when manoeuvrability is the main concern. When raw speed is the focus, use Speed as the test limit instead. Routine flight normally requires no test; difficult manoeuvres, bad visibility, confined airspace, and dangerous terrain can require Vehicle Tests.

Take-Off and Landing
Most fixed-wing aircraft require an airfield or a long stretch of flat ground. VTOL craft are the main exception.

Travel
Rotorcraft average about 220 km/h over open terrain and 140 km/h in urban airspace; rural tilt-wing craft can reach about 300 km/h. Prop planes average about 250 km/h, small jets about 1,000 km/h, and large jets about 800 km/h over long distances.

subcategories.Bikes:
Skill
Use Pilot Ground Craft; the Bike specialization applies. Most bikes are available with electric or hybrid biofuel engines.

Road and Off-Road Ratings
Where Handling or Speed is written as two values separated by a slash, use the first value On Road and the second Off Road.

Vehicle Tests
Roll Pilot Ground Craft + Reaction [Handling], or use Speed as the limit when raw speed is the key factor.
subcategories.Cars:
Skill
Use Pilot Ground Craft; the Wheeled specialization applies. Most cars are available with electric or hybrid biofuel engines.

Road and Off-Road Ratings
Where Handling or Speed is written as two values separated by a slash, use the first value On Road and the second Off Road.

Vehicle Tests
Roll Pilot Ground Craft + Reaction [Handling], or use Speed as the limit when raw speed is the key factor.
subcategories.Trucks and Vans:
Skill
Use Pilot Ground Craft; the Wheeled specialization applies to the Core Rulebook trucks and vans. Most are available with electric or hybrid biofuel engines.

Road and Off-Road Ratings
Where Handling or Speed is written as two values separated by a slash, use the first value On Road and the second Off Road.

Vehicle Tests
Roll Pilot Ground Craft + Reaction [Handling], or use Speed as the limit when raw speed is the key factor.
subcategories.Boats:
Skill
Use Pilot Watercraft. The Motorboat or Ship specialization may apply depending on the craft.

Vehicle Tests
Roll Pilot Watercraft + Reaction [Handling], or use Speed as the limit when raw speed is the key factor.

Travel
Currents and the shape of rivers, lakes, and coastlines can substantially alter travel time. Surface craft generally cannot travel in a perfectly straight line between two points.
subcategories.Submarines:
Skill
Use Pilot Watercraft; the Submarine specialization applies.

Vehicle Tests
Roll Pilot Watercraft + Reaction [Handling], or use Speed as the limit when raw speed is the key factor.

Operating Environment
Submarines follow the normal watercraft rules, while the depth, currents, visibility, confined underwater spaces, and other hazards can increase Vehicle Test difficulty at the gamemaster's discretion.
subcategories.Fixed-Wing Aircraft:
Skill
Use Pilot Aircraft; the Fixed-Wing specialization applies.

Take-Off and Landing
Most fixed-wing aircraft require an airfield or a long stretch of flat ground to take off or land. Individual aircraft may be amphibious or have other exceptions described in their own entry.

Vehicle Tests
Roll Pilot Aircraft + Reaction [Handling], or use Speed as the limit when raw speed is the key factor.
subcategories.Rotorcraft:
Skill
Use Pilot Aircraft; the Rotary Wing specialization applies to helicopters.

Travel
Helicopters and tilt-wing aircraft average about 220 km/h over open terrain and 140 km/h in urban airspace. They can usually travel in a straight line between locations, with fuel being a major practical limitation.

Vehicle Tests
Roll Pilot Aircraft + Reaction [Handling], or use Speed as the limit when raw speed is the key factor.
subcategories.VTOL/VSTOL:
Skill
Use Pilot Aircraft. The Vectored Thrust or Tilt Wing specialization applies depending on the craft.

Take-Off and Landing
VTOL and VSTOL craft are designed for vertical or short take-off and landing, allowing them to operate without the full runway normally required by fixed-wing aircraft.

Travel
Tilt-wing aircraft can average about 300 km/h in rural airspace. Other VTOL craft use their own Speed rating and normal aircraft rules.

Vehicle Tests
Roll Pilot Aircraft + Reaction [Handling], or use Speed as the limit when raw speed is the key factor.
